problem: Every morning when Tim wakes up, he groggily searches around his sock drawer and picks two socks randomly. If he has 10 gray-bottomed socks and 8 white-bottomed socks in his drawer, what is the probability that he picks a matching pair?

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of socks Tim has in his drawer? How did you arrive at this number?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many socks does Tim pick at a time? Why is this information important?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many ways are there for Tim to choose 2 socks out of the total number of socks? What formula can be used to calculate this?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many gray-bottomed socks does Tim have? How many ways are there for him to choose 2 gray-bottomed socks?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many white-bottomed socks does Tim have? How many ways are there for him to choose 2 white-bottomed socks?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why did we calculate the number of ways for Tim to choose 2 gray-bottomed socks and 2 white-bottomed socks separately? What is the significance of this?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many ways are there for Tim to choose a matching pair of socks? How did you calculate this?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the probability of Tim picking a matching pair of socks? How did you derive this probability?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Could there be other methods or formulas that can be used to solve this problem? Can you think of any alternative approaches?
classified_questions.label: e
